summary: R2 systems architecture review accepts M4-GRAIN-SUPPLY-001. The prior REQUEST_CHANGES blockers are fixed: reserve, consume, and release now emit explicit grain supply DomainEventV1 records; reserve and release emit M2 population group deltas for actual stock changes; consumedAmount plus lossAmount conservation is explicit with unmet demand recorded as deterministic shortage; loss-only and loss overflow cases no longer silently drop demand; and malformed duplicate GrainSupplyReservationId rows spanning campaign scopes are rejected by runtime validation and command behavior. No new R3 architecture blocker was found.

tests_run.result: 1 test file passed; 7 tests passed. Covers reserve/consume/release events, M2 deltas, conservation, deterministic allocation/hash stability, loss-only consumption, loss overflow shortage accounting, forecast immutability, negative command cases, malformed quantities, and cross-campaign duplicate reservation IDs.
Node hash 61e0ba1c matched Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; command/query hash d275faa4 matched; save/load hash 28074edd matched; save/load byte length 10920.
format:check, lint, workspace typecheck, and architecture:check passed.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic canaries matched; root vitest 28 files/187 tests passed; web vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

tests_run.result: No matches in relevant diff for nondeterministic APIs, UI/platform imports, forbidden scope terms, or explicit any usage.

decisions: ACCEPT. The R2 diff stays within the task's allowed paths and product scope: protocol, sim-core, tests, task state, and outbox handoff only.

Reserve, consume, and release use the shared GameCommand protocol path for player and AI actors. Authority checks are tied to campaign ownership and do not introduce separate player-only or AI-only execution paths.

Grain conservation is auditable. Reserve deducts controlled M2 stock into M4 carried reservations; consume moves carried grain into consumed/loss/shortage accounting without mutating M2 stock; release returns carried grain to M2 stock and marks reservation rows released.

Deterministic source allocation is preserved through sorted controlled source pools and stable tie-breakers. Shortage remainder is assigned to a deterministic final source row, and reason ordering is covered by tests.

Canonical hash, runtime validation, saved event parsing, and query copy behavior were reviewed. Grain supply reservations participate in canonical world-state formatting and validation, forecast query runs on cloned state, and new event records round-trip through existing sim-core event-tail parsing.

No premature route capacity, march execution, siege or battle resolution, UI, save-format milestone work, full market economy, naval logistics, server, Rust/WASM, or new dependency work was introduced.
risks: Save-format milestone work remains intentionally out of scope. The current change extends sim-core runtime state and event-tail parsing only; future save-format milestones still need their own DTO/versioning review if M4 grain reservations become part of milestone persistence policy.

The reserve event summarizes source count and total reserved grain while per-source detail remains in authoritative reservation rows and M2 deltas. This is acceptable for this bounded task but should not be treated as a complete logistics audit stream for future route capacity work.
requested_action: Lead orchestrator may route this accepted R2 implementation to qa_reviewer or the next integration gate per workflow. Do not mark CLOSED until required downstream QA/integration checks complete.
